embeds.description:
## 💾 GAME FOLDER BACKUP STRATEGIES

Backing up a large game install without eating double your drive space.

-# ─────────────────────────────────
## ✅ Best Option — Back Up Only the Crack Files

You don't need to copy the entire game folder. The files that get overwritten by updates or online fixes are small — back these up:
- `ms_emu.json`
- `Enable_Invites_FH6.bat`
- Contents of the `NoDVD/` folder

That's a few MB max. If an update breaks online, these are what you restore.

-# ─────────────────────────────────
## 📋 Full Backup Options

**Robocopy /MIR** — incremental sync
```
robocopy "C:\Game" "D:\Backup\Game" /MIR /Z /MT:8
```
First run copies everything. Subsequent runs only transfer changed files — fast and space-efficient after the first time.

**7-Zip LZMA2** — compressed archive
Game files compress **30–50%** with LZMA2. A 143 GB folder becomes ~70–90 GB. Slow to create, good for archiving a clean install.
Right-click folder → *7-Zip → Add to archive* → Format: `7z`, Method: `LZMA2`, Level: `Normal`.

**VSS / Previous Versions** (Windows built-in, zero setup)
Right-click any folder → *Properties → Previous Versions*. Windows already runs VSS snapshots by default — near-instant, shares unchanged blocks so space used is proportional to what actually changed. Best for rolling back after messing with mods or config.

**Hard links** (same NTFS drive only)
`robocopy /MIR /SL` or FastCopy — if the backup destination is on the same NTFS drive, hard links let you have two apparent copies that share the same disk blocks until one file changes.

-# ─────────────────────────────────
## 🎮 For Mod Safety Specifically

**Use VSS (Previous Versions)** — no extra tools, near-zero space for small changes, already running on most systems.
Right-click your game folder → *Properties → Previous Versions* → take a snapshot before modding. Roll back anytime from that same menu.
